---
id: "13.5.10"
title: "Splunk RUM Front-End JavaScript Error Rate per Page, Browser, Release, and Regression Detection"
criticality: "high"
splunkPillar: "Observability"
---

# UC-13.5.10 · Splunk RUM Front-End JavaScript Error Rate per Page, Browser, Release, and Regression Detection

## Description

Detects client-side JavaScript failure spikes and regressions from Splunk RUM and OpenTelemetry browser telemetry: unhandled window errors, unhandled promise rejections, captured console failures, SDK-reported splunk.error events, fingerprint clusters from error.message plus stack top frame, per-page and per-route impact, browser and release cohort attribution, probable PII or token leakage in exception strings, and chronic third-party noise patterns that should be filtered.

## Value

Product and reliability leaders see browser defects while they are still concentrated in specific routes, releases, and browser versions, which protects conversion and support costs. Privacy and security stakeholders receive early warning when exception text crosses redaction policy. Engineers gain Sentry-style grouping signals inside Splunk so triage starts with fingerprints instead of thousands of duplicate lines.

## Implementation

Land index=rum sourcetype=rum:error plus rum:session or rum:metric denominators, mirror otel_browser exceptions, refresh frontend_release_inventory.csv and rum_pii_redaction_rules.csv lookups, accelerate Performance summaries for the tstats arm when used. Save observability_uc_13_5_10_rum_js_error_fingerprints_release_regression every fifteen minutes with earliest=-6h, route critical privacy rows to privacy on-call, high severity to frontend platform, archive weekly samples.

## Detailed Implementation

Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with frontend platform engineering, the observability pipeline team that forwards Splunk Observability Real User Monitoring into Splunk Enterprise indexes, privacy counsel reviewing client-side telemetry, and site reliability engineers who operate release automation. This use case is deliberately not UC-13.5.9 Core Web Vitals work: that sibling scores Largest Contentful Paint, Interaction to Next Paint, Cumulative Layout Shift, and paint-oriented budgets. Here the signal is client-side JavaScript failure modes captured from real browsers: unhandled exceptions surfaced through the window error event pipeline, unhandled promise rejections, console.error style instrumentation hooks where your agent records them, and explicitly reported faults emitted through the Splunk RUM Web SDK error API that developers call when they catch and rethrow structured failures. The goal is to quantify how often those failures occur per normalized page URL, per semantic route name, per browser name and version tuple, per release_version cohort, and per fingerprint cluster derived from error message text plus the top stack frame, then to detect sudden spikes, deploy-shaped regressions, privacy-policy violations when messages leak emails or bearer tokens, and chronic noise from third-party scripts that should be filtered before paging.

Scope boundaries keep sibling observability use cases complementary rather than duplicative. UC-13.5.1 through UC-13.5.4 remain application performance monitoring trace duration, trace error rate, trace completeness, and related server-side narratives. UC-13.5.7 remains RED service map saturation reasoning about service graphs. UC-13.5.9 remains Core Web Vitals and high-level JavaScript error rate blended into paint and interaction budgets. None of those replace the drill path this document prescribes: deep grouping of browser-side exceptions, fingerprint cardinality, release cohort attribution, and privacy leakage checks on exception strings. If you only watch server traces, you will still miss bundle regressions, feature-flag fallout, hydration mismatches, polyfill gaps, cross-origin script failures, and single-page application router exceptions that never become a five hundred response on an origin you control.

Measurement semantics start with stable identifiers. The page.url field should represent the customer-visible URL without secrets in query strings; strip tokens at the collector when privacy rules require it. The route.name or http.route style key should track logical journeys inside single-page applications so impact rolls up to product surfaces rather than opaque hash routes. The release_version field must align with your continuous deployment tags so a regression search can compare the previous good cohort to the newly deployed cohort without manual spreadsheet joins. Browser.name and browser.version should be parsed from user_agent when the agent does not already populate them so Safari versus Chrome regressions remain obvious. Session.id is the denominator guard: error rate per session is often more actionable than raw event counts when traffic volume doubles during marketing campaigns. Device.type and os.name segment phone versus desktop failures. Geo.country explains regional third-party outages. Page.referrer helps identify embedded experiences and unexpected entry paths.

Fingerprinting follows a Sentry-style or Bugsnag-style grouping heuristic for operational triage rather than pretending one hash replaces full symbolicated stacks. Concatenate normalized error.message with the first line of error.stack or the inferred stack_top_frame after source map resolution when you operate a private symbolication service. Hash with a stable function such as md5 after lowercasing and trimming so capitalization noise does not split incidents. Track dc(err_fingerprint) separately from raw err_events so a single looping defect does not look like dozens of unrelated outages. Teach engineers to keep exception messages short and structured; log correlation identifiers instead of raw user emails.

Privacy prerequisites are strict. Maintain rum_pii_redaction_rules.csv as an allowlist and policy table that documents which regular expressions are permitted to appear in client exception strings, which fields must be dropped at ingest, and which teams must be paged when a probable secret appears. Maintain frontend_release_inventory.csv with release_version, deploy_utc timestamps, git_sha, deploy_owner, and optional browser_matrix notes so Splunk can join deploy facts without querying five separate CI systems during an incident. Legal review should confirm retention on error payloads, whether stack traces may contain internal repository paths, and how quickly you must purge messages that accidentally captured regulated data.

Risk briefing: a spike in JavaScript errors after a deploy may be correct behavior if the new release finally surfaces a latent validation bug that users always hit; pair error trends with support tickets and checkout funnel metrics before rolling back solely on noise_share. Conversely, a flat error rate can hide misery if sessions collapse because the agent stopped loading; monitor session volume lanes alongside error lanes. Third-party advertisements and tag managers routinely emit ResizeObserver loop limit exceeded, Script error without detail on cross-origin frames, and CORS policy noise that should be tagged and filtered rather than treated as application defects.

Differentiation recap: browser JavaScript error telemetry with fingerprint clustering, release cohort regression logic, privacy leakage detection, third-party noise awareness, route impact attribution, and explicit coverage of SDK-reported errors, not Core Web Vitals thresholds, not server-side trace latency alone, not service graph RED saturation by itself.

Operational prerequisites include Content Security Policy alignment for the RUM snippet, HTTPS everywhere the agent runs, and agreement on sampling rates so statistical variance does not masquerade as incidents. Document how splunk.error or the documented manual error capture path is used for handled errors that still represent customer-visible defects.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up five complementary lanes that multisearch can fuse in Step 3: raw Splunk RUM error events, RUM session or metricized session volume for denominators, OpenTelemetry browser exception logs landing in index=otel_browser, curated frontend release metadata from frontend_release_inventory.csv, and a Performance data model tstats lane that proves CIM-aligned summaries still move when you need a coarse volume sanity check during indexer incidents.

Primary RUM errors belong in index=rum with sourcetype=rum:error when you normalize Observability exports into that convention; if your forwarder still emits vendor sourcetypes, map them with props.conf FIELDALIAS and EVAL so error.message, error.type, error.stack, page.url, page.referrer, browser.name, browser.version, release_version, session.id, user_agent, os.name, device.type, geo.country, and route.name exist predictably. Capture window.onerror and unhandledrejection sources distinctly when the agent exposes event type fields so you can filter or weight them differently. Ensure console.error capture is optional and policy-controlled so noisy logging does not drown real defects.

Session denominator events belong in sourcetype=rum:session or rum:metric rows that include session identifiers and page.url stamps at least once per interval; if you only have spans, derive sessions with a scheduled search that materializes dc(session.id) per fifteen minutes per page_url into a summary index keyed for joins. Low session counts should suppress alerts in downstream logic so a dozen errors during a quiet night do not page executives.

OpenTelemetry browser SDK errors should land in index=otel_browser with compatible field aliases for error.message and error.stack so the same fingerprint evals work without forked SPL. Align service.name, deployment.environment, and service.version with release_version when possible so dual-agent estates still compare apples to apples.

The frontend_release_inventory.csv lookup must be regenerated on every production deploy with immutable version identifiers. Store UTC timestamps in ISO-8601 form, normalize release_version strings to match the agent tag, and include an owner column for paging. The rum_pii_redaction_rules.csv lookup should list policy_slug keys, human readable rx_allowlist_note guidance, and optional contact fields for privacy escalation; Splunk join keys on policy_slug with a constant rum_default row so searches never fail when the table is small.

props.conf guidance: set TRUNCATE high enough for stack traces, use LINE_BREAKER only if multiplexed payloads arrive, and TIMESTAMP_FIELDS aligned to agent time. transforms.conf can route noisy vendor domains into a quarantine index for experimentation without deleting evidence.

Pre-save validation: index=rum sourcetype=rum:error earliest=-1h must return non-zero counts in production-like tenants after snippet deployment; rum:session must show plausible session volumes; otel_browser must mirror a lab thrown exception; inputlookup frontend_release_inventory.csv must include the current release row; tstats against Performance must either return rows when acceleration is on or be disabled intentionally with a documented exception.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as observability_uc_13_5_10_rum_js_error_fingerprints_release_regression with a fifteen minute schedule during steady production, dispatch earliest=-6h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is critical or high after calibration in Step 4. Throttle duplicate page_url, browser_kv, release_version triples for thirty minutes unless severity escalates from high to critical. Include recommended_action, lanes_seen, and fp_distinct in pager narratives so responders open the right runbook without rerunning ad hoc searches.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans the rum:error arm, the rum session denominator arm, the otel_browser error arm, the generating lookup arm for release inventory metadata, and the tstats Performance volume arm so a silent single lane does not hide a genuine browser outage. coalesce ladders absorb dotted versus flattened JSON field naming drift across agent versions. stats buckets errors per fifteen minutes per page_url, browser_kv, and release_version, then join enriches deploy_owner and git_sha from frontend_release_inventory.csv. streamstats measures short momentum on err_rate_pct per tuple so sudden spikes stand out against the immediately prior bucket. eventstats estimates cohort mean and standard deviation by release_version so a regression tied to a new build is visible even when the absolute percent still looks small. join to rum_pii_redaction_rules.csv carries governance text for privacy reviewers. case maps composite signals into severity and recommended_action tokens suitable for routing.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.5.10 RUM browser JS error spikes, fingerprints, deploy regression, PII leakage hints, third-party noise. Tunables earliest=-6h bucket=15m")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=rum sourcetype=rum:error earliest=-6h latest=now
      | eval page_url=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('page.url', page_url, url_path, "/unknown"))))
      | eval route_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('route.name', route_name, 'http.route', ""))))
      | eval err_msg=toString(coalesce('error.message', error_message, message, ""))
      | eval err_type=toString(coalesce('error.type', error_type, exception_type, ""))
      | eval err_stack=toString(coalesce('error.stack', error_stack, stack_trace, ""))
      | eval stack_top=if(len(err_stack)>0, mvindex(split(err_stack, "\n"),0), "")
      | eval err_fingerprint=md5(lower(trim(strcat(err_msg, "||", stack_top))))
      | eval browser_kv=strcat(lower(trim(toString(coalesce('browser.name', browser_name, "")))), " ", toString(coalesce('browser.version', browser_version, "")))
      | eval release_version=toString(coalesce(release_version, release, app_version, "unknown"))
      | eval session_id=toString(coalesce('session.id', session_id, ""))
      | eval user_agent=toString(coalesce(user_agent, 'user_agent.original', ""))
      | eval os_name=toString(coalesce('os.name', os_name, ""))
      | eval device_type=toString(coalesce('device.type', device_type, ""))
      | eval geo_country=toString(coalesce('geo.country', geo_country, country, ""))
      | eval page_referrer=toString(coalesce('page.referrer', referrer, ""))
      | eval row_type="err"
      | eval hit=1
      | eval lane="rum_error"
      | fields _time page_url route_name err_msg err_type err_fingerprint stack_top browser_kv release_version session_id user_agent os_name device_type geo_country page_referrer row_type hit lane ]
    [ search index=rum (sourcetype=rum:session OR sourcetype=rum:metric) earliest=-6h latest=now
      | eval page_url=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('page.url', page_url, url_path, "/unknown"))))
      | eval sid=toString(coalesce('session.id', session_id, ""))
      | bin _time span=15m
      | stats dc(sid) AS sessions_seen BY _time page_url
      | eval row_type="sess"
      | eval hit=0
      | eval lane="rum_session"
      | eval route_name=""
      | eval err_fingerprint=""
      | eval browser_kv="*"
      | eval release_version="*"
      | fields _time page_url route_name sessions_seen row_type hit lane ]
    [ search index=otel_browser earliest=-6h latest=now
      | eval page_url=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('page.url', page_url, url_path, "/unknown"))))
      | eval route_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('route.name', route_name, 'http.route', ""))))
      | eval err_msg=toString(coalesce('error.message', exception_message, message, ""))
      | eval err_type=toString(coalesce('error.type', exception_type, ""))
      | eval err_stack=toString(coalesce('error.stack', exception_stacktrace, ""))
      | eval stack_top=if(len(err_stack)>0, mvindex(split(err_stack, "\n"),0), "")
      | eval err_fingerprint=md5(lower(trim(strcat(err_msg, "||", stack_top))))
      | eval browser_kv=strcat(lower(trim(toString(coalesce('browser.name', browser_name, "")))), " ", toString(coalesce('browser.version', browser_version, "")))
      | eval release_version=toString(coalesce(release_version, service_version, "unknown"))
      | eval session_id=toString(coalesce('session.id', session_id, ""))
      | eval user_agent=toString(coalesce(user_agent, 'user_agent.original', ""))
      | eval os_name=toString(coalesce('os.name', os_name, ""))
      | eval device_type=toString(coalesce('device.type', device_type, ""))
      | eval geo_country=toString(coalesce('geo.country', geo_country, country, ""))
      | eval page_referrer=toString(coalesce('page.referrer', referrer, ""))
      | eval row_type="err"
      | eval hit=1
      | eval lane="otel_browser_err"
      | fields _time page_url route_name err_msg err_type err_fingerprint stack_top browser_kv release_version session_id user_agent os_name device_type geo_country page_referrer row_type hit lane ]
    [ | inputlookup frontend_release_inventory.csv
      | eval app_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(app_name, product, ""))))
      | eval release_version=toString(coalesce(release_version, build_id, ""))
      | eval deploy_ts=if(len(trim(toString(coalesce(deploy_utc, deployed_at, ""))))>10 AND match(trim(toString(coalesce(deploy_utc, deployed_at, ""))), "^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-"), strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(deploy_utc, deployed_at, ""))), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), null())
      | eval git_sha=toString(coalesce(git_sha, commit, ""))
      | eval row_type="relmeta"
      | eval hit=0
      | eval lane="fe_deploy_lookup"
      | eval _time=coalesce(deploy_ts, now())
      | fields _time app_key release_version git_sha row_type hit lane ]
    [ | tstats summariesonly=t count FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-6h latest=now BY _time span=15m
      | eval row_type="perfvol"
      | eval hit=0
      | eval lane="tstats_perf"
      | eval page_url="/aggregate/web_volume_proxy"
      | eval perf_events=count
      | fields _time page_url perf_events row_type hit lane ]
| eval page_url=coalesce(page_url, "/unknown")
| eval browser_kv=coalesce(browser_kv, "unknown unknown")
| eval release_version=coalesce(release_version, "unknown")
| where row_type="err" OR row_type="sess"
| bin _time span=15m
| stats
    sum(hit) AS err_events
    dc(err_fingerprint) AS fp_distinct
    dc(session_id) AS affected_sessions
    values(err_type) AS err_types_seen
    values(err_msg) AS err_msgs
    values(lane) AS lanes_seen
    first(route_name) AS route_name
    max(sessions_seen) AS sessions_bucket
  BY _time page_url browser_kv release_version
| eval sessions_seen=coalesce(sessions_bucket, affected_sessions, 0)
| eval err_rate_pct=if(sessions_seen>0, round(100*err_events/nullif(sessions_seen,0), 3), err_events)
| join type=left max=0 release_version
    [| inputlookup frontend_release_inventory.csv
     | eval release_version=toString(coalesce(release_version, build_id, ""))
     | fields release_version deploy_owner git_sha ]
| sort 0 + page_url browser_kv release_version + _time
| streamstats window=2 current=t global=f last(err_rate_pct) AS prev_rate BY page_url browser_kv release_version
| eventstats avg(err_rate_pct) AS env_avg_err_rate stdev(err_rate_pct) AS env_stdev_err BY release_version
| eval spike_z=if(env_stdev_err>0, round((err_rate_pct-env_avg_err)/env_stdev_err, 3), 0)
| eval pii_policy="rum_default"
| join type=left max=0 pii_policy
    [| inputlookup rum_pii_redaction_rules.csv
     | eval pii_policy=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(policy_slug, "rum_default"))))
     | fields pii_policy rx_allowlist_note ]
| eval err_blob=mvjoin(err_msgs, " | ")
| eval err_sample=substr(err_blob,1,800)
| eval pii_viol=if(match(err_sample, "(?i)(bearer\\s+[a-z0-9\\-\\._~\\+/]+=*)|([a-z0-9._%+-]+@[a-z0-9.-]+\\.[a-z]{2,})"), 1, 0)
| eval noise_share=if(match(err_sample, "(?i)(ResizeObserver loop limit exceeded|blocked by extension|CORS policy|Script error)"), 1, 0)
| eval severity=case(
    pii_viol==1, "critical",
    err_rate_pct>=5 AND spike_z>=3, "high",
    err_rate_pct>=2 AND coalesce(prev_rate,0)<1 AND spike_z>=2, "high",
    err_rate_pct>=1.5, "medium",
    fp_distinct>=12 AND err_events>=50, "medium",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical", "halt_client_log_export_scrub_messages_rotate_tokens_review_rum_privacy_rules",
    severity=="high" AND noise_share==0, "diff_release_cohort_check_source_maps_rollback_or_hotfix",
    severity=="high", "filter_third_party_noise_validate_cors_and_tag_whitelist",
    severity=="medium", "triage_top_fingerprints_open_ticket_per_route_owner",
    true(), "monitor_dashboard_keep_sampling_stable")
| table _time page_url route_name browser_kv release_version err_events sessions_seen err_rate_pct fp_distinct pii_viol noise_share spike_z severity recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[observability_uc_13_5_10_rum_js_error_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -6h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_5_10_rum_js_error_fingerprints_release_regression | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance guidance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above internal objectives, materialize fifteen-minute rollups into a summary index keyed on page_url, browser_kv, release_version, and err_fingerprint, widen alert searches to earliest=-2h on the summary, and retain this full search for investigations. Keep high-cardinality stack traces off alert subject lines.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in Splunk Observability Real User Monitoring error views for the same window. Pick a page you understand, filter to JavaScript errors, and compare event counts and top messages to the Splunk table row for the same page_url, browser_kv, and release_version. Reconcile differences by checking forwarding delay, subsampling, or field alias drift before blaming application code.

Validate fingerprint stability by selecting one noisy defect, confirming err_fingerprint remains stable across multiple sessions when message and top frame match, and confirming distinct defects receive distinct fingerprints when messages differ. Validate release joins by comparing git_sha from Splunk to your deployment tracker for the same release_version.

Validate privacy logic by injecting a synthetic exception message containing a fake email in a lab tenant, confirming pii_viol flags critical, then removing the test payload. Validate noise_share by loading known third-party CORS and ResizeObserver samples and confirming recommended_action routes to filtering guidance rather than hotfix.

Validate session denominators by comparing sessions_seen to Observability session charts for the same route. If denominators are systematically low, fix session ingestion before trusting err_rate_pct.

Negative test: on a quiet maintenance window with no deploys, critical severity should be rare once thresholds are calibrated, proving the alert is not trivially misfiring on sparse traffic.

Runbook linkage: attach links to your source map server, your feature-flag console, your third-party tag catalog, and your privacy escalation path.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Deploy-shaped regression concentrated in a new release_version cohort: compare err_rate_pct and fp_distinct between previous and current release using side-by-side dashboards, roll forward or back under change control, and bisect feature flags before reopening generic infrastructure tickets.

Case 2 — Single fingerprint explodes but Core Web Vitals stay flat: treat as pure JavaScript defect, open a Sev-2 to frontend owners, attach symbolicated stacks, and verify splunk.error breadcrumbs for handled paths that still harm users.

Case 3 — Cross-origin Script error without stacks: enable CORS headers for static assets where policy allows, document vendor limitations, and downgrade severity when noise_share indicates classic opaque cross-origin patterns.

Case 4 — ResizeObserver loop limit exceeded storms from ads: tag third-party ownership, filter known vendors in alerting predicates, and negotiate creative contracts that forbid resize loops without size hints.

Case 5 — Browser extension blocked script errors: segment by user_agent anomalies, publish internal guidance for clean profiles on corporate apps, and avoid paging application teams when extension signatures dominate.

Case 6 — PII leakage in error.message triggers critical: freeze dashboards for privacy review, rotate any exposed tokens, patch logging to hash identifiers, and update rum_pii_redaction_rules.csv with the new forbidden pattern.

Case 7 — OpenTelemetry browser lane diverges from Splunk RUM lane: reconcile field aliases, verify duplicate agents are not double-firing, and pick a single source of truth for paging while the other remains investigative.

Case 8 — Session denominator collapse makes err_rate_pct look infinite: fix rum:session ingestion first, suppress alerts when sessions_seen falls below your documented floor, and page the pipeline team instead of frontend.

Case 9 — Route.name blank so impact hides inside page_url hash routes: patch router instrumentation, rebuild baselines, and revalidate owner lookups keyed on route.name for product rollups.

Case 10 — tstats Performance arm goes quiet while errors spike: treat as acceleration or search peer issue, verify datamodel acceleration health, and fall back to raw session searches until summaries recover.

Case 11 — Canary release emits expected elevated errors: annotate change_ticket_id on events, require dual approval before paging, and compare canary cohort size against production volume guards.

Case 12 — Everything looks healthy yet support tickets climb: widen time range, inspect splunk.error manual reports, and validate sampling did not drop the failing cohort from visibility.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists rum:error, rum session, otel_browser, release lookup, and tstats arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, and inputlookup appear; final table lists fourteen analyst-visible columns including severity and recommended_action.

## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.5.10 RUM browser JS error spikes, fingerprints, deploy regression, PII leakage hints, third-party noise. Tunables earliest=-6h bucket=15m")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=rum sourcetype=rum:error earliest=-6h latest=now
      | eval page_url=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('page.url', page_url, url_path, "/unknown"))))
      | eval route_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('route.name', route_name, 'http.route', ""))))
      | eval err_msg=toString(coalesce('error.message', error_message, message, ""))
      | eval err_type=toString(coalesce('error.type', error_type, exception_type, ""))
      | eval err_stack=toString(coalesce('error.stack', error_stack, stack_trace, ""))
      | eval stack_top=if(len(err_stack)>0, mvindex(split(err_stack, "\n"),0), "")
      | eval err_fingerprint=md5(lower(trim(strcat(err_msg, "||", stack_top))))
      | eval browser_kv=strcat(lower(trim(toString(coalesce('browser.name', browser_name, "")))), " ", toString(coalesce('browser.version', browser_version, "")))
      | eval release_version=toString(coalesce(release_version, release, app_version, "unknown"))
      | eval session_id=toString(coalesce('session.id', session_id, ""))
      | eval user_agent=toString(coalesce(user_agent, 'user_agent.original', ""))
      | eval os_name=toString(coalesce('os.name', os_name, ""))
      | eval device_type=toString(coalesce('device.type', device_type, ""))
      | eval geo_country=toString(coalesce('geo.country', geo_country, country, ""))
      | eval page_referrer=toString(coalesce('page.referrer', referrer, ""))
      | eval row_type="err"
      | eval hit=1
      | eval lane="rum_error"
      | fields _time page_url route_name err_msg err_type err_fingerprint stack_top browser_kv release_version session_id user_agent os_name device_type geo_country page_referrer row_type hit lane ]
    [ search index=rum (sourcetype=rum:session OR sourcetype=rum:metric) earliest=-6h latest=now
      | eval page_url=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('page.url', page_url, url_path, "/unknown"))))
      | eval sid=toString(coalesce('session.id', session_id, ""))
      | bin _time span=15m
      | stats dc(sid) AS sessions_seen BY _time page_url
      | eval row_type="sess"
      | eval hit=0
      | eval lane="rum_session"
      | eval route_name=""
      | eval err_fingerprint=""
      | eval browser_kv="*"
      | eval release_version="*"
      | fields _time page_url route_name sessions_seen row_type hit lane ]
    [ search index=otel_browser earliest=-6h latest=now
      | eval page_url=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('page.url', page_url, url_path, "/unknown"))))
      | eval route_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce('route.name', route_name, 'http.route', ""))))
      | eval err_msg=toString(coalesce('error.message', exception_message, message, ""))
      | eval err_type=toString(coalesce('error.type', exception_type, ""))
      | eval err_stack=toString(coalesce('error.stack', exception_stacktrace, ""))
      | eval stack_top=if(len(err_stack)>0, mvindex(split(err_stack, "\n"),0), "")
      | eval err_fingerprint=md5(lower(trim(strcat(err_msg, "||", stack_top))))
      | eval browser_kv=strcat(lower(trim(toString(coalesce('browser.name', browser_name, "")))), " ", toString(coalesce('browser.version', browser_version, "")))
      | eval release_version=toString(coalesce(release_version, service_version, "unknown"))
      | eval session_id=toString(coalesce('session.id', session_id, ""))
      | eval user_agent=toString(coalesce(user_agent, 'user_agent.original', ""))
      | eval os_name=toString(coalesce('os.name', os_name, ""))
      | eval device_type=toString(coalesce('device.type', device_type, ""))
      | eval geo_country=toString(coalesce('geo.country', geo_country, country, ""))
      | eval page_referrer=toString(coalesce('page.referrer', referrer, ""))
      | eval row_type="err"
      | eval hit=1
      | eval lane="otel_browser_err"
      | fields _time page_url route_name err_msg err_type err_fingerprint stack_top browser_kv release_version session_id user_agent os_name device_type geo_country page_referrer row_type hit lane ]
    [ | inputlookup frontend_release_inventory.csv
      | eval app_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(app_name, product, ""))))
      | eval release_version=toString(coalesce(release_version, build_id, ""))
      | eval deploy_ts=if(len(trim(toString(coalesce(deploy_utc, deployed_at, ""))))>10 AND match(trim(toString(coalesce(deploy_utc, deployed_at, ""))), "^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-"), strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(deploy_utc, deployed_at, ""))), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), null())
      | eval git_sha=toString(coalesce(git_sha, commit, ""))
      | eval row_type="relmeta"
      | eval hit=0
      | eval lane="fe_deploy_lookup"
      | eval _time=coalesce(deploy_ts, now())
      | fields _time app_key release_version git_sha row_type hit lane ]
    [ | tstats summariesonly=t count FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-6h latest=now BY _time span=15m
      | eval row_type="perfvol"
      | eval hit=0
      | eval lane="tstats_perf"
      | eval page_url="/aggregate/web_volume_proxy"
      | eval perf_events=count
      | fields _time page_url perf_events row_type hit lane ]
| eval page_url=coalesce(page_url, "/unknown")
| eval browser_kv=coalesce(browser_kv, "unknown unknown")
| eval release_version=coalesce(release_version, "unknown")
| where row_type="err" OR row_type="sess"
| bin _time span=15m
| stats
    sum(hit) AS err_events
    dc(err_fingerprint) AS fp_distinct
    dc(session_id) AS affected_sessions
    values(err_type) AS err_types_seen
    values(err_msg) AS err_msgs
    values(lane) AS lanes_seen
    first(route_name) AS route_name
    max(sessions_seen) AS sessions_bucket
  BY _time page_url browser_kv release_version
| eval sessions_seen=coalesce(sessions_bucket, affected_sessions, 0)
| eval err_rate_pct=if(sessions_seen>0, round(100*err_events/nullif(sessions_seen,0), 3), err_events)
| join type=left max=0 release_version
    [| inputlookup frontend_release_inventory.csv
     | eval release_version=toString(coalesce(release_version, build_id, ""))
     | fields release_version deploy_owner git_sha ]
| sort 0 + page_url browser_kv release_version + _time
| streamstats window=2 current=t global=f last(err_rate_pct) AS prev_rate BY page_url browser_kv release_version
| eventstats avg(err_rate_pct) AS env_avg_err_rate stdev(err_rate_pct) AS env_stdev_err BY release_version
| eval spike_z=if(env_stdev_err>0, round((err_rate_pct-env_avg_err)/env_stdev_err, 3), 0)
| eval pii_policy="rum_default"
| join type=left max=0 pii_policy
    [| inputlookup rum_pii_redaction_rules.csv
     | eval pii_policy=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(policy_slug, "rum_default"))))
     | fields pii_policy rx_allowlist_note ]
| eval err_blob=mvjoin(err_msgs, " | ")
| eval err_sample=substr(err_blob,1,800)
| eval pii_viol=if(match(err_sample, "(?i)(bearer\\s+[a-z0-9\\-\\._~\\+/]+=*)|([a-z0-9._%+-]+@[a-z0-9.-]+\\.[a-z]{2,})"), 1, 0)
| eval noise_share=if(match(err_sample, "(?i)(ResizeObserver loop limit exceeded|blocked by extension|CORS policy|Script error)"), 1, 0)
| eval severity=case(
    pii_viol==1, "critical",
    err_rate_pct>=5 AND spike_z>=3, "high",
    err_rate_pct>=2 AND coalesce(prev_rate,0)<1 AND spike_z>=2, "high",
    err_rate_pct>=1.5, "medium",
    fp_distinct>=12 AND err_events>=50, "medium",
    true(), "low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical", "halt_client_log_export_scrub_messages_rotate_tokens_review_rum_privacy_rules",
    severity=="high" AND noise_share==0, "diff_release_cohort_check_source_maps_rollback_or_hotfix",
    severity=="high", "filter_third_party_noise_validate_cors_and_tag_whitelist",
    severity=="medium", "triage_top_fingerprints_open_ticket_per_route_owner",
    true(), "monitor_dashboard_keep_sampling_stable")
| table _time page_url route_name browser_kv release_version err_events sessions_seen err_rate_pct fp_distinct pii_viol noise_share spike_z severity recommended_action
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load latest(Performance.mem_used) AS mem_used FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-6h latest=now BY Performance.dest
| rename Performance.dest AS perf_dest
| eval route_hint=lower(perf_dest)
| join type=left max=0 route_hint [| inputlookup frontend_release_inventory.csv | eval route_hint=lower(toString(coalesce(canary_host, app_name, ""))) | fields route_hint release_version ]
| table perf_dest cpu_load mem_used route_hint release_version
```

## Visualization

Severity-colored table by page_url, browser_kv, and release_version with sparklines for err_rate_pct, parallel bar for fp_distinct, badges for pii_viol and noise_share, drilldowns to raw rum:error and otel_browser stacks, and cohort compare views across release_version before and after deploy_ts.
